ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Continued To Jun-05-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alan Moss, 415-730-1453, Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Stephen Ellingson, Esq. 650-637-9100 of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson Guslani Simonson & Clause LLP for Defendant STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Plaintiff argues and cites case. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company's motion to set aside default is granted. "The court may, upon any terms as may be just, relieve a party or his or her legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her through his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (Code Civ. Proc. 473(b).) Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) is liberally construed to prevent injustice and allow cases to be decided on their merits. (Frank E. Beckett Co. v. Bobbit (1960) 180 Cal.App.2d Supp. 921, 924.) State Farm has explained that it was actively investigating Plaintiff's uninsured motorist claim that had been submitted to arbitration and that the arbitration caption was similar to this litigation. (Struble Decl. 6.) Therefore, State Farm's claims department mistakenly thought the complaint pertained to the uninsured motorist claim and did not forward it to defense counsel until the default judgment was later discovered. (Id. 7-8.)

Even where a default is attributable to a neglect of duty, such mistakes may nevertheless be excusable for purposes of Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) where the other party was not prejudiced. (See Benjamin v. Dalmo Mfg. Co. (1948) 31 Cal.2d 523, 527, 531 [holding that affidavits from a company president and secretary explaining their failure to read or comprehend a summons constituted excusable neglect].) Plaintiff has made no showing that he will be prejudiced by the Court setting aside this default. The Court declines to rule on Defendant's evidentiary objections as they are not germane to its ruling on this motion.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
